title: Recursive Problem Solving
summary: Implement and explain a set of recursive functions that demonstrate base cases, decomposition, and call stack reasoning.
studentInstructions: Create a Python file named recursion_solutions.py.
Implement recursive solutions for sum_list(values), reverse_string(text), count_occurrences(values, target), and is_palindrome(text).
Do not use loops inside the four required recursive functions.
Create explanation.md explaining the base case, recursive case, and input reduction for each function.
Include at least three test examples per function either in a main block or in a separate test section of the Python file.
Make sure your code handles empty inputs and one-item inputs where applicable.

rubric.description: All four functions return correct results and use recursion as the primary solution strategy.
Each function has appropriate base cases and guarantees progress toward termination without infinite recursion.
The solution handles empty inputs, one-item inputs, repeated values, mixed case or spacing for palindrome checks when documented, and other relevant boundary cases.
The explanation clearly identifies base cases, recursive cases, and how each recursive call reduces the problem.
The submission includes meaningful tests or examples for each function, including at least one edge case per function.

gradingInstructions: Use only this assignment and its rubric.
Check required files before grading quality.
